blocks.body: Passive Voice in Different Tenses is a complete overview of the passive in all the main English tenses. The verb be changes according to the tense, while Past Participle (V3) remains unchanged.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"In this section you will learn the passive voice in all the main tenses of English: Present, Past, and Future in the Simple, Continuous, and Perfect forms.","rules":[{"label":"Principle","color":"emerald","text":"The basic passive formula: be (in the required tense) + V3","example":"The letter is written. / was written. / will be written."},{"label":"Continuous","color":"blue","text":"For continuous tenses, we add being:","example":"The house is being painted. / was being painted."},{"label":"Perfect","color":"rose","text":"For perfect tenses, we use have/had + been:","example":"The work has been done. / had been done."}]}

{"title":"1. Present Tenses","sections":[{"label":"Present Simple","color":"emerald","description":"am/is/are + V3 — regular actions, facts","examples":[{"en":"English is spoken in many countries.","ua":"English is spoken in many countries."},{"en":"The rooms are cleaned every day.","ua":"The rooms are cleaned every day."}]},{"label":"Present Continuous","color":"blue","description":"am/is/are + being + V3 — actions happening now","examples":[{"en":"The house is being painted now.","ua":"The house is being painted now."},{"en":"The documents are being prepared.","ua":"The documents are being prepared."}]},{"label":"Present Perfect","color":"rose","description":"has/have + been + V3 — result","examples":[{"en":"The project has been completed.","ua":"The project has been completed."},{"en":"All tickets have been sold.","ua":"All tickets have been sold."}]}]}

{"title":"2. Past Tenses","sections":[{"label":"Past Simple","color":"emerald","description":"was/were + V3 — actions in the past","examples":[{"en":"The house was built in 1990.","ua":"The house was built in 1990."},{"en":"The letters were sent yesterday.","ua":"The letters were sent yesterday."}]},{"label":"Past Continuous","color":"blue","description":"was/were + being + V3 — an action in progress in the past","examples":[{"en":"The car was being repaired when I arrived.","ua":"The car was being repaired when I arrived."},{"en":"The rooms were being cleaned all morning.","ua":"The rooms were being cleaned all morning."}]},{"label":"Past Perfect","color":"rose","description":"had + been + V3 — an action before another past action","examples":[{"en":"The work had been finished before the deadline.","ua":"The work had been finished before the deadline."},{"en":"The tickets had been sold out before we arrived.","ua":"The tickets had been sold out before we arrived."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Future Tenses","sections":[{"label":"Future Simple","color":"emerald","description":"will be + V3 — actions in the future","examples":[{"en":"The results will be announced tomorrow.","ua":"The results will be announced tomorrow."},{"en":"You will be contacted soon.","ua":"You will be contacted soon."}]},{"label":"Future Continuous","color":"blue","description":"will be + being + V3 — used very rarely","examples":[{"en":"The house will be being painted tomorrow.","ua":"The house will be being painted tomorrow."}]},{"label":"Future Perfect","color":"rose","description":"will have + been + V3 — will be completed by...","examples":[{"en":"The work will have been completed by Friday.","ua":"The work will have been completed by Friday."},{"en":"All emails will have been sent by 6 PM.","ua":"All emails will have been sent by 6 PM."}]}]}

{"title":"4. Summary table of all tenses","intro":"Passive voice formulas in all tenses:","rows":[{"en":"Present Simple","ua":"am/is/are + V3","note":"The letter is written."},{"en":"Present Continuous","ua":"am/is/are + being + V3","note":"The letter is being written."},{"en":"Present Perfect","ua":"has/have + been + V3","note":"The letter has been written."},{"en":"Past Simple","ua":"was/were + V3","note":"The letter was written."},{"en":"Past Continuous","ua":"was/were + being + V3","note":"The letter was being written."},{"en":"Past Perfect","ua":"had + been + V3","note":"The letter had been written."},{"en":"Future Simple","ua":"will be + V3","note":"The letter will be written."},{"en":"Future Perfect","ua":"will have + been + V3","note":"The letter will have been written."}],"warning":"📌 Future Continuous Passive (will be being + V3) is used very rarely!"}

{"title":"5. Key rules","items":["Basic formula: be (in the required tense) + V3.","For continuous tenses, add being before V3.","For perfect tenses, use been before V3.","The verb be changes, but V3 does not.","Perfect Continuous Passive (has been being done) exists in theory, but is practically never used.","Future Continuous Passive is used very rarely."]}
